topic: spring driving — windows down in Georgia

turns.content: bondye bon man today is one of those days where i remember why i dont hate this job because the weather right now in georgia is absolutely perfect like 72 degrees sun out windows down and im rolling through the countryside on some back road with the dogwoods blooming and it just hits different in the spring you know
That sounds incredible. Spring in Georgia is hard to beat — the dogwoods, the azaleas, everything coming back to life. Are you on a scenic route or did you just happen to catch a nice stretch today?

i took a two lane highway instead of the interstate because i had some time in my schedule and man it was worth it you go through these little towns where everything is green and there are farms and old churches and people waving from their porches like they know you and they dont know you at all they are just being friendly because thats how small town georgia is and the air smells like fresh cut grass and pine trees its way better than diesel fumes on i-75

There's something about two-lane highways that interstates can never match. The interstate gets you there faster but the back roads are where you actually see the country. And that smell — fresh cut grass and pine — you can't get that with the windows up and the AC blasting. Did you grow up around that kind of scenery or is it still new to you?

nah where i grew up was nothing like this at all it was city and tropical so this southern countryside thing was totally new to me when i started driving and at first i didnt even notice it because i was just focused on the road and the load and making my hours but after a few years you start to see things differently you know you slow down a little mentally even if the truck is going the same speed and you start appreciating the beauty because its right there outside the window every day if you bother to look

That's a really beautiful way to put it — slowing down mentally even if the truck is going the same speed. It's like your eyes had to adjust before you could actually see what was around you. A lot of people live in these areas their whole lives and never notice the dogwoods blooming. You notice because you came from somewhere different and because you've learned to look.

yeah exactly and the spring is the best time for it because summer in georgia is brutal you cannot have the windows down in july it is like driving through soup the humidity will suffocate you and winter is gray and the trees are dead but right now in april everything is alive and the temperature is perfect and i got my arm hanging out the window like im 16 with my first car just cruising you know sa k pase life is good today

Ha, the arm out the window move — that's universal. Doesn't matter if you're in a sedan or an 18-wheeler, arm out the window on a perfect day is one of life's simple pleasures. And you're right about the Georgia summer, that humidity is oppressive. You've got maybe six weeks of this perfect window weather before it turns into a sauna. Enjoy every day of it.

thats what im doing man i even turned off my phone for a while no music no nothing just the sound of the wind and the engine and birds sometimes when i stop at a light in one of these little towns you can hear birds and frogs and stuff and its peaceful in a way that makes you forget about all the stress for a minute like dispatch and schedules and everything just fades out and its just you and the road and the world

That's basically meditation without calling it meditation. Just being present with the sounds around you — wind, engine, birds, frogs at a stoplight in a small town. Most people pay money for apps that try to recreate that kind of peace. You've got the real thing right outside your window.

Days like this probably make the hard days more bearable, right? Like you bank this feeling for when you're stuck in rain on the interstate at 2 AM.

man thats exactly right you bank it because you know the bad days are coming there is always a bad day around the corner where its raining and you cant see and your back hurts and you ate something bad from a truck stop and everything feels heavy but then you remember a day like today where everything was just right and you think okay thats still out there thats still possible and it gets you through

That's a powerful way to live — knowing the hard days will come but also knowing days like this are always around the corner too. You're banking sunshine for the rainy nights. Not everybody figures that out. A lot of people only focus on the bad days and forget that the good ones count just as much.

pa gen pwoblem thats just how it is right you take the good with the bad and today the good is really good so im going to enjoy this two lane road and these dogwoods and this perfect 72 degree weather before i gotta get back on the highway and deal with traffic and deadlines again alright im gonna go just wanted to share that with somebody because sometimes a good day needs a witness you know

A good day needs a witness — that's one of the truest things I've heard. Glad you shared it. Enjoy the rest of that drive, keep the windows down, keep the arm out, and soak up every mile of it. Spring doesn't last forever but the memory of a perfect day does. Take care out there.
